text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a software development practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Whenever new code is submitted, a CI pipeline automatically triggers a build and runs a series of tests. This process helps teams catch issues early, before they become larger problems in the final product. Automated builds ensure that the code compiles correctly, while tests check for bugs and regressions. By catching issues early, teams can fix them quickly and avoid delays later. CI pipelines make collaboration smoother and help maintain high software quality.
